dialogueChunks.intro: Two of our most respected figures are at each other's throats, {player_name}. {quest_ally} and {target_npc} both believe they should lead, and neither will yield.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both have their supporters, and {quest_focus} has split the community down the middle. The {enemy_type} have taken note of our distraction: raids are increasing near {location_hint} while we bicker.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone outside the dispute needs to act while our leaders compete. Will you handle the real threats while we sort out {quest_stakes}?

playerResponses.text: I will handle what matters while they argue.
Maybe I can help settle this too.

dialogueChunks.intro: Two of our artisans have been feuding for months, {player_name}. It has gotten so bad that {quest_ally} refuses to work on the same projects as {target_npc}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both claim superiority and they have dragged the whole settlement into their rivalry over {quest_focus}. Now they have proposed a challenge, and both are looking for outside help to gather {quest_item} from {location_hint}.
dialogueChunks.outro: It is petty, but it is disrupting everything. If you gather the materials they need, we might end {quest_threat} and get our people cooperating again. {quest_reward_hint} is on the table.

playerResponses.text: I will gather what is needed.
Artisans and their egos. Fine, I will help.
Let them sort out their own squabble.

dialogueChunks.intro: {quest_ally} challenged {target_npc} to a contest: who can clear more {enemy_type} from the land around {location_hint}. What started as bravado has become dangerously reckless.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Both are taking bigger risks to outdo the other, venturing deeper into hostile territory. {quest_focus} has become less about protecting the settlement and more about pride. Someone is going to get killed {quest_time_pressure}.
dialogueChunks.outro: I need someone out there who can actually thin the numbers while keeping those two from getting themselves killed. {player_name}, are you up for it?

playerResponses.text: I will show them both how it is done.
Someone has to be the responsible one.
Let them learn the hard way.
